title: [Gyarados] Battle Guide
summary: A [Water]/[Flying] setup sweeper whose [moxie] and [dragon-dance] combine into one of the tier's most terrifying snowball win conditions.
strengths: [dragon-dance] boosts Attack and Speed in one turn
[moxie] stacks Attack on every KO after setup
[Water]/[Flying]/[Dark] cove[rage] hits almost every metagame core
weaknesses: Low damage output pre-setup — commits to one turn

recommendedMoves.reason: Reliable 100 accuracy [Water] STAB with flinch chance
One-turn Attack and Speed boost that defines the role

recommendedItems.reason: Recovery for long games and setup
Absorbs one status move to guarantee the setup turn

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Gyarados] is a [Water]/[Flying] physical setup sweeper built around the combination of [moxie] and [dragon-dance]. A base 125 Attack and 81 Speed line looks ordinary, but one [dragon-dance] pushes its effective Speed into the 120 tier and its Attack past every wall in the format. The [moxie] ability turns every follow-up KO into another boost, which is what makes [Gyarados] a win condition rather than just another mid-game attacker.

## Strengths
The [moxie] plus [dragon-dance] loop is the most volatile snowball. A single setup turn flips the speed race, and each subsequent KO stacks another Attack boost on top. The [Water]/[Flying] typing grants a [Ground] immunity and strong coverage angles, and [crunch] covers the [Psychic] and [Ghost] walls that would otherwise blank [waterfall].

## Weaknesses & Answers
The 4x [Electric] weakness is the defining structural problem. A single [thunderbolt] from any reasonable special attacker instantly removes [Gyarados] from the game, so you cannot commit the setup turn unless the electric threats are removed or baited out. [stealth-rock] chips for 25 percent on every entry and compounds the [Rock] weakness, so hazard control is mandatory team support. Without a clean setup turn [Gyarados] just isn't threatening enough to justify the slot.

## Recommended Moveset
[waterfall] is the locked [Water] STAB. [crunch] is mandatory to punish [Ghost] and [Psychic] walls that [waterfall] can't crack. [earthquake] is the coverage slot that answers [Electric] and [Steel] attackers. The fourth slot is [dragon-dance], since the whole strategy depends on getting one boost. [ice-fang] is an alternate coverage pick if you expect [Ground] or [Dragon] teams.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default. [lum-berry] is an alternate pick that makes sure one status move (like a [will-o-wisp] or [thunder-wave]) doesn't ruin the setup turn. The ability must be [moxie]. Tera [Flying] maxes STAB damage, while Tera [Ground] is the defensive option that erases the 4x [Electric] weakness.

## Teammates & Synergy
A hazard remover ([rapid-spin] or [defog]) is mandatory for [Gyarados] to run its setup plan. A dedicated electric answer. Adding a status spreader that can [toxic] or burn walls in advance weakens the opposing bench so [Gyarados]'s first coverage move can KO, and a fast cleaner on the back line finishes any Pokemon left alive after the [moxie] chain ends.

## Play Patterns
The main plan is to force a switch. Post-boost, the correct attack is often [crunch] or [earthquake] before [waterfall], because those answer the specific wall the opponent brought in. Save the Tera for the moment a [Rock] or [Electric] move is telegraphed; using it defensively almost always secures the sweep.
